content.problemStatement:
You have been recently hired as a developer in CodeChef. Your first mission is to implement a feature that will determine the number of submissions that were judged late in a contest.

There are NNN submissions, numbered 111 through NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th submission was submitted at time SiS_iSi​ and judged at time JiJ_iJi​ (in minutes). Submitting and judging both take zero time. Please determine how many submissions received their verdicts after a delay of more than 555 minutes.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of submissions. Then N lines follow, each containing two integers Si and Ji — the submission time and the judging time of the i‑th submission.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer — the number of submissions whose judging delay (Ji - Si) is strictly greater than 5 minutes.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Delays are 2,0,6,10,5 minutes. Only the 3rd and 4th submissions have delay >5, so the answer is 2.
First test case: delays 0,6,6 → 2 submissions exceed 5. Second test case: delays 6,0,5,6 → 2 submissions exceed 5.
All delays are exactly 4 minutes, none exceed 5, so the answer is 0.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the N submissions, compute the difference d = Ji - Si and increment a counter when d > 5. After processing all submissions output the counter. This uses only a constant amount of extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — each submission is examined once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — only a few integer variables are used.
